GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "This new haircare brand promises to bring the salon to you", "If you've ever struggled to find affordable haircare products that promise salon quality results, then you'll be relieved to learn you're not alone. When faced with the exact same problem, New Zealand-born entrepreneur and beauty enthusiast Jaimee Lupton set her sights on creating the ultimate solution. Introducing MONDAY, a cruelty-free haircare brand that was designed to bring the salon to you. Free of parabens and sodium lauryl sulfate, the MONDAY range–which boasts shampoos and conditioners for those looking to smooth, repair or service their sensitive locks–is here to help you maintain that perfect mane from the comfort of your own home with effective and innovative new formulations. "I'm a huge believer that every person, no matter their story, should be entitled to look and feel good. The lack of transparency and extortionate pricing across the beauty sector is what gave me the idea to start MONDAY," says Lupton, who is committed to revolutionising the haircare game with this new line of accessible products. When launching MONDAY, Lupton enlisted the help of fellow New Zealander and childhood friend, model Georgia Fowler, who is the new muse of the haircare brand. In addition to her role as the face of MONDAY, the former Victoria's Secret model is an energised partner who has also helped Lupton with the creative direction of the brand. "Over the course of my career, I've been lucky enough to be exposed to quality haircare and have noticed an immense difference in my hair quality since using these products. MONDAY stood out to me because we share the belief that salon quality products should be made more accessible," says Fowler. "With my busy work and travel schedule, my hair's durability is tested all the time and demands to look done most days of the week," adds the model, who has worked with the likes of Chanel, Prada and Miu Miu. "I chose to partner with MONDAY because I've noticed visible results since using it as part of my routine." In addition to Fowler, the haircare brand has unveiled a series of 'MONDAY Muses' who will be letting you in on all they've learnt about both business and beauty. These muses include indigenous model and former Vogue Australia cover star Samantha Harris, celebrated fashion designer Maggie Marilyn, renowned beautician Kristin Fisher and founder of An Organised Life, Beck Wadworth. "Something that really resonated with me about MONDAY was Jaimee's mission to make luxury accessible to all women," says Marilyn. "This is something I felt really passionate about when creating our 'Somewhere' line; that customers could buy a quality product, something that was going to empower them, at an affordable price point." Complete with 11 amino acids and a number of essential plant extracts, MONDAY haircare products contain innovative formulations that feature a mix of low irritants and strong surfactants, with this combination promising to protect both your hair and your scalp, while simultaneously delivering a salon quality clean. On top of that, each bottle is crafted from recycled plastic in an effort to minimise the MONDAY's carbon footprint and reduce landfill. So for salon quality haircare products that promise to leave your hair feeling healthy, soft and shiny without costing you more than necessary, look no further than MONDAY–which just so happens to also look chic on your bathroom shelf. Shop MONDAY haircare products in store at Coles."
